No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or objectives links to poverty eradication.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops advanced language and writing skills, contributing to relevant skills for employment and literacy (Targets 4.4, 4.6).

equip students with writing skills at the intermediate level

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or objectives links to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or objectives links to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or objectives links to energy.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or objectives links to industry, innovation, or infrastructure.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Fostering an 'international perspective' (Core Competence D) and 'fundamental knowledge in Southeast Asian studies' (Core Competence B) can contribute to understanding between peoples, a very indirect link to Target 10.2.
evidence: To obtain fundamental knowledge in Southeast Asian studies

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or objectives links to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The instruction to 'Respect Copyright' is a very tangential link to responsible consumption of intellectual property, but does not align with the core focus of SDG 12 on material consumption, waste, and resource efficiency.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or objectives links to climate change or action.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or objectives links to marine life or oceans.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or objectives links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course explicitly requires students to 'Respect Copyright' (維護智慧財產權), which supports the rule of law concerning intellectual property (Target 16.3).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course builds capacity for international cooperation by teaching a foreign language and fostering an 'international perspective' (Core Competence D), supporting knowledge sharing and capacity building (Targets 17.6, 17.9).
evidence: To obtain fundamental knowledge in Southeast Asian studies
